class_schedule.topic: Internship Orientation and Professional Development Goals
learning_objectives: Understand the academic requirements, grading criteria, and deliverables for the internship course
Set professional development goals aligned with digital communications career aspirations
Establish communication protocols with faculty advisor and site supervisor

lecture_notes.introduction: This course bridges your academic training and professional career in digital communications. Your internship is a real job with real expectations, and this weekly seminar provides structure for reflecting on your experience and building professional skills alongside it.
key_concepts: Internship requirements: minimum 20 hours/week at an approved digital communications organization
Weekly journal expectations: structured reflection connecting daily work to academic frameworks
Communication protocol: bi-weekly check-ins with faculty advisor, regular updates to site supervisor
Professional development goals using the SMART framework tailored to digital communications competencies
Ethical obligations: NDA compliance, professional conduct, representing the university and the profession
lecture_notes.demonstration: Goal-setting workshop: each student drafts three SMART professional development goals for their internship and receives peer and faculty feedback, focusing on specific digital communications skills they want to develop
setup_requirements: Signed internship agreement on file with the department
Contact information for site supervisor submitted to faculty advisor
Digital journal set up shared with faculty advisor

review.value: Goals should target specific professional competencies: 'Develop proficiency in Google Analytics by independently building and presenting a monthly analytics report by week 8' is SMART; 'get better at analytics' is not
All course deliverables must be reviewed for NDA compliance before submission; generalize proprietary details while still demonstrating professional learning and contribution

learning_objectives: Navigate the first weeks of a professional digital communications environment
Apply effective communication strategies for cross-functional collaboration
Identify and adapt to organizational tools, processes, and culture
readings: Harvard Business Review, 'How to Make the Most of Your Internship'
Selected blog posts on transitioning from student to communications professional (provided on LMS)

lecture_notes.introduction: Your first weeks set the tone for your entire internship. In digital communications specifically, every organization has its own tool stack, approval processes, and brand voice conventions. Learning these quickly while demonstrating value is the primary challenge of the early weeks.
key_concepts: Onboarding in digital communications: learning the tool stack, brand guidelines, approval workflows, and content calendar
Cross-functional relationships: working with design, product, sales, and leadership as a communications professional
Platform access and permissions: managing credentials, accessing analytics, and understanding team roles
Asking effective questions: demonstrating initiative while seeking guidance appropriately
Building credibility: delivering small wins early to earn trust for larger responsibilities
lecture_notes.demonstration: Round-table discussion: students share their first-week experiences, challenges encountered, and strategies that worked, with faculty facilitating problem-solving for common digital communications workplace issues
setup_requirements: Initial observations documented in weekly journal
List of tools and platforms used at internship site

review.value: Every digital communications team uses a different combination of tools (Hootsuite vs. Sprout Social, Mailchimp vs. HubSpot, GA4 vs. Adobe Analytics); learning new tools quickly is a career-long requirement, and the internship is your first practice

Identify a small, visible contribution you can make in the first two weeks: fixing a typo on the website, suggesting an improvement to a social post, or organizing a shared drive; small wins build trust faster than grand plans

class_schedule.topic: Applying Academic Frameworks to Professional Practice
learning_objectives: Identify connections between academic concepts and daily professional tasks
Develop strategies for translating classroom frameworks into workplace recommendations
Document professional learning in portfolio-ready format
readings: Selected readings connecting academic theory to practice (provided on LMS)
Student-selected readings from prior courses relevant to their internship work

lecture_notes.introduction: One of the most valuable things you can demonstrate as an intern is the ability to connect academic knowledge to practical problems. When you can articulate why a campaign should be structured a certain way using strategic frameworks, you add value beyond execution.
key_concepts: Theory-practice connection: using course frameworks (PESO, AIDA, content strategy) to inform workplace recommendations
Professional recommendations: framing suggestions using data, frameworks, and projected outcomes rather than personal preference
Portfolio documentation: translating daily work into portfolio-ready case studies and work samples
Learning gap identification: recognizing where academic preparation prepared you well and where professional practice differs
Continuous learning plan: identifying skills to develop based on professional experience
lecture_notes.demonstration: Framework application workshop: each student identifies one academic framework relevant to their current internship work, explains how it applies, and presents a specific recommendation to their team based on the framework
setup_requirements: Current internship project or challenge for analysis

review.value: Academic frameworks give you vocabulary and structure for professional recommendations; saying 'I recommend this based on SMART objectives and the PESO model' is more persuasive than 'I think we should try this'
Document your internship work in portfolio format: the challenge, your approach, the frameworks applied, the outcome, and what you learned. This transforms daily work into career assets.

learning_objectives: Assess progress toward professional development goals established at the start of the internship
Articulate the most significant professional lessons learned in digital communications practice
Adjust goals and strategies for the second half of the internship
readings: Mid-term reflection prompt document (provided on LMS)
Dweck, Mindset, Ch. 3 (selected sections on growth in professional contexts)

lecture_notes.introduction: The midpoint is time for honest assessment. What skills have you developed? What surprised you about professional digital communications? What do you want to prioritize in the remaining weeks? This reflection directly informs your final report and career planning.
key_concepts: Goal progress review: measuring against SMART objectives set in week 1 with specific evidence
Unexpected learning: digital communications skills and insights you did not anticipate gaining
Industry reality check: how professional practice differs from academic expectations
Goal revision: adjusting targets based on actual experience and emerging opportunities
Supervisor feedback: soliciting and incorporating constructive criticism productively
lecture_notes.demonstration: Mid-term presentation: each student presents a 5-minute summary of their internship experience, key learnings, and adjusted goals, followed by faculty and peer feedback
setup_requirements: Mid-term reflection essay completed and submitted before class

review.value: Most interns report that professional digital communications is faster-paced, more collaborative, more data-driven, and more focused on business outcomes than they expected from coursework; naming this gap is the first step to closing it
Asking your supervisor 'What is one specific digital communications skill I should focus on improving in the next 7 weeks?' yields more actionable feedback than 'How am I doing?'

learning_objectives: Translate internship accomplishments into portfolio-ready documentation
Prepare for professional employment in digital communications
Identify transferable skills developed during the internship

lecture_notes.introduction: Your internship has produced real work experience that becomes a career asset only if you document and present it effectively. This session focuses on transforming your internship into portfolio pieces, resume content, and interview stories.
key_concepts: Portfolio documentation under NDA: describing skills demonstrated and problems solved without revealing proprietary details
Resume update: translating internship responsibilities into achievement-oriented bullet points with metrics
Interview stories: preparing STAR-format answers (Situation, Task, Action, Result) based on internship experiences
LinkedIn and professional presence: updating profiles with internship experience and skills
Networking maintenance: keeping professional relationships active after the internship ends
lecture_notes.demonstration: Portfolio workshop: students practice writing NDA-compliant case studies of their internship work and converting internship experiences into resume bullet points and interview stories
setup_requirements: List of internship accomplishments and contributions

review.value: 'Managed the company's Instagram account, growing followers by 35% and engagement rate from 2.1% to 4.8% over three months through a revised content strategy' is far more compelling than 'Managed social media accounts'

Situation (context), Task (your responsibility), Action (what you specifically did), Result (measurable outcome); every internship experience can be structured this way for behavioral interview questions

learning_objectives: Deliver a professional presentation summarizing the internship experience and outcomes
Articulate a clear post-graduation career strategy informed by internship experience
Provide constructive feedback to peers on their internship presentations
readings: Review all weekly journal entries for synthesis

lecture_notes.introduction: Your final presentation synthesizes fifteen weeks of professional digital communications experience into a cohesive narrative. This is practice for the conversations you will have in interviews when asked about your industry experience.
key_concepts: Presentation structure: context, contributions, challenges, growth, career impact
Quantifying contributions: metrics, projects delivered, campaigns launched, tools mastered
Career direction: how the internship clarified or changed your professional goals
Professional relationship maintenance: staying connected after the internship ends
Continuous development: identifying the next skills to develop based on career direction
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each student delivers a 10-minute final presentation to faculty and peers, followed by 5 minutes of Q&A. Faculty provide feedback on both content and presentation skills.
setup_requirements: Thank-you notes drafted for internship supervisor and colleagues

review.value: Frame your internship as a growth story: what you knew coming in, what challenged you, how you rose to those challenges, and how the experience shaped your professional direction in digital communications
The professional relationships from your internship are career assets; a brief thank-you note and quarterly check-ins maintain connections for references, collaborations, and future opportunities

major_assignments.title: Internship Plan
major_assignments.description: Develop a structured internship plan defining professional development goals, communication protocols, and learning objectives specific to digital communications competencies you intend to develop.
objectives: Define three to five SMART professional development goals
Establish communication protocols with supervisor and faculty advisor
Research the organization's digital communications practices and competitive position
Create a learning plan for tools and skills required by the role
deliverables: Internship plan document (4-5 pages) with SMART goals, communication plan, and learning objectives
Skills gap analysis mapping current digital communications abilities against role requirements
Signed internship agreement and supervisor contact information
Initial weekly journal entry documenting expectations and first impressions

criteria: Goals are SMART and focused on specific digital communications competencies
Goals reflect genuine professional development aspirations
At least one goal addresses a known skill gap
Goals are achievable within the internship timeframe
Communication protocols are clearly defined
Organization research demonstrates genuine effort
Skills gap analysis is honest and specific
Learning plan includes concrete resources and milestones
Document is professionally written and formatted
All required forms are completed
Initial journal entry is reflective and substantive
Plan demonstrates mature professional expectations
SMART goals targeting specific digital communications competencies
Thorough preparation with organization research and skills gap analysis
Professional documentation quality
Clear communication plan with all parties

major_assignments.title: Mid-Term Reflection
major_assignments.description: Write a comprehensive mid-term reflection assessing progress toward professional development goals with specific evidence from your digital communications work, documenting key learnings and adjusting your plan.
objectives: Assess progress toward each SMART goal with specific evidence
Document the most significant professional lessons learned
Identify unexpected challenges and how they were addressed
Revise goals for the remaining weeks
deliverables: Mid-term reflection essay (5-7 pages) with goal assessment, learnings, and revised plan
Updated SMART goals with revisions justified

criteria: Honest assessment of progress with specific evidence
Digital communications skills growth is documented with examples
Challenges are discussed thoughtfully
Self-awareness is demonstrated throughout
Revisions reflect lessons from the first half
New goals remain SMART and achievable
Strategy adjustments are practical
Revisions are justified with reasoning
Reflection connects daily tasks to professional development
Industry observations demonstrate growing awareness
Supervisor feedback is integrated thoughtfully
Lessons are generalizable beyond the specific internship
Presentation is clear and within time limit
Key learnings are communicated effectively
Responses to questions are thoughtful
Genuine engagement with the experience is evident
Evidence-based self-assessment of digital communications growth
Thoughtful goal revision demonstrating adaptability
Professional insight connecting experience to career development
Effective communication of internship experience

major_assignments.title: Final Internship Report and Presentation
major_assignments.description: Produce a comprehensive final report synthesizing your entire internship experience, including professional growth documentation, portfolio additions, and career impact assessment. Deliver a formal presentation summarizing your internship journey.
objectives: Synthesize the full internship experience into a professional narrative
Document specific contributions and portfolio-worthy work
Assess the internship's impact on career direction and professional readiness
Deliver a polished presentation demonstrating professional communication skills
deliverables: Final report (8-12 pages) covering contributions, growth, challenges, and career impact
Portfolio additions: at least two NDA-compliant case studies from the internship
Updated resume incorporating internship experience with achievement-oriented bullets
10-minute formal presentation to faculty with Q&A
Thank-you correspondence drafted for supervisor and colleagues

criteria: Comprehensive narrative covering the full internship arc
Specific contributions documented with evidence of impact
Challenges discussed honestly with lessons learned
Career impact assessment is grounded in experience
Case studies are substantive and NDA-compliant
Skills demonstrated and problems solved are articulated
Work is presented in portfolio-ready format
Resume is updated with compelling achievement bullets
Presentation is well-structured and within time limit
Contributions and growth are communicated effectively
Q&A responses demonstrate deep reflection
Professional communication skills are evident
All deliverables are proofread and polished
Thank-you correspondence is professional and personalized
Portfolio demonstrates readiness for professional employment
Overall package reflects career-ready professionalism
Comprehensive final report synthesizing a rich internship experience
Portfolio-ready work samples demonstrating professional contributions
Polished presentation showcasing communication skills
Professional package demonstrating career readiness

internship_performance.description: Supervisor evaluation of work quality and professionalism

weekly_journals.description: Weekly reflection journals documenting learning and growth

final_report.description: Comprehensive internship report with portfolio additions

presentation.description: Final presentation to faculty summarizing internship experience

course_policies.attendance: Attendance at the weekly Friday seminar is mandatory. More than 2 unexcused absences result in grade reduction. Internship site attendance follows the agreed schedule with your supervisor.
course_policies.late_submissions: Weekly journals are due every Friday by 5 PM. Late journals lose 10% per day. Major assignment deadlines are firm; extensions require advance arrangement.
course_policies.collaboration: All deliverables are individual work reflecting your personal internship experience. Peer feedback during seminar discussions is encouraged.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All written work must be your own. Internship work in reports must comply with your NDA. Consult your supervisor before including proprietary information.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students need a digital journal platform, document editing software, and presentation tools. All deliverables submitted through LMS.
